item.title: Simulating Stopping Traffic Waves with Autonomous Vehicles
item.description:
Can autonomous vehicles help reduce traffic jams by stopping traffic waves?

Inspired by a thought from a coworker who had heard of this before, and to practice more LLM development, I ran some simulations based on Stern et al’s 2018 paper: Dissipation of stop-and-go waves via control of autonomous vehicles: Field experiments. I attempted to replicate the results in simulation and extend to 3 lanes of traffic with drivers changing lanes.

item.title: Nurofen FeverSmart Temperature Monitor Teardown
item.description:
The Nurofen FeverSmart Temperature Monitor is a wearable real-time temperature sensor for small children and babies, which adheres to the child’s skin under the armpit and provides real time measurements of temperature to a companion smartphone, via a base/relay station.

This was recently on sale at a significant discount (£14.99 vs RRP £49.99) at my local TK Maxx, and it appears Nurofen might have discontinued the product. Being interested in small bluetooth devices, I bought one to take apart.

item.title: Dedicating a GPU for CUDA on Ubuntu 18.04
item.description:
Recently, I have been doing some deep learning on Tensorflow and PyTorch by dual booting my PC into Ubuntu 18.04 and training with CUDA drivers.

However, I am using that graphics card to display my desktop as well, and the desktop becomes annoyingly laggy when running training, especially in GPU accelerated applications like Firefox or POLAR. Changing to using the internal graphics would fix this.

In addition, it would be nice to claim back the graphics RAM used by X.Org, by dedicating the GPU to CUDA use only 1.

item.title: Using watchdog with LEDs on OrangePi Zero
item.description:
I have a small OrangePi zero on my home network turn on my desktop PC using Wake-on-Lan to SSH in and grab files/run processing when I am out and about. I use zerotier so I can SSH into both the orangepi and my desktop without needing Dynamic DNS or opening ports in my router.

However, the SD card used is a bit unreliable, and so occasionally the device will lock up. It would be nice to detect this automatically and reboot when it happens. The board also has some LEDs, which we could use to show the health of the system as well.

item.title: Converting Folder of WAV Files to FLAC
item.description:
For audio research, speech and noise corpus’s are commonly stored in uncompressed format, generally WAV. This results in very large file sizes, for instance the CSTR VCTK Corpus is 10GB.

However, common compression formats like MP3 or Ogg Vorbis would cause distortion of noise signals rendering them useless for comparision against results obtained from the original files.

Conversion

As an exercise, I wrote this script to convert these all into the lossless FLAC format, using FFmpeg and GNU Parallel. Unfortunately (see below), some files refuse to convert losslessly, but I still consider this some good practice in shell programming.

item.title: Home
item.description: This is a personal blog about electronics, machine learning, and whatever else might interest me. Opinions are my own unless otherwise stated.

item.title: Site Credits
item.description:
Site:

  • Built using Hugo
  • Theme is Cocoa Enhanced
  • Hosted on Github Pages

Images:

  • 404 Logo: lost by KEN MURRAY from the Noun Project
  • Favicon: Sound by icon 54 from the Noun Project
